preview_text:
Spain enters as a heavy favourite with a 71% win probability according to the model, leaving Uruguay with just an 8% chance of victory. The gap between the two teams is substantial, positioning Spain as a clear frontrunner in this matchup on neutral ground.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 21%, suggesting that while Spain should control much of the match, Uruguay has enough defensive structure to avoid defeat outright. The spread between a Spain win and a draw is relatively narrow—about 49 percentage points—indicating that competitive resistance from Uruguay is baked into the forecast, even if an away victory remains unlikely.

The model leans notably more confident in Spain than the market consensus does. Sportsbooks price Spain at 63% and place Uruguay at 15%, a gap of roughly 8 percentage points favouring Uruguay relative to the model's assessment. Polymarket sentiment aligns more closely with the model. The dashboard takes no position on which source is correct; the divergence may reflect different risk assessments or market-specific liquidity dynamics.
